title: Compared to Estimates, McCormick (MKC) Q2 Earnings: A Look at Key Metrics
text: While the top- and bottom-line numbers for McCormick (MKC) give a sense of how the business performed in the quarter ended May 2023, it could be worth looking at how some of its key metrics compare to Wall Street estimates and year-ago values.

title: Private Label Trade-Down Slows as Grocery Inflation Decelerates, McCormick Says
text: As year-over-year grocery inflation normalizes, lapping last year's spikes, consumers continue to cut back where possible, spices and seasonings giant McCormick observes, but many grocery shoppers are beginning to relax.

title: McCormick Rebound Imminent: Executive Transition Complete
text: McCormick's NYSE: MKC price action wobbled following a better-than-expected Q2 and increased guidance. The news proves that the decision to transition Brenden Foley into the CEO position was good.

title: McCormick (MKC) Beats Q2 Earnings Estimates
text: McCormick (MKC) came out with quarterly earnings of $0.60 per share, beating the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $0.57 per share. This compares to earnings of $0.48 per share a year ago.

title: McCormick beats profit expectations and lifts full-year outlook, while sales comes up a bit shy
text: McCormick & Co. Inc. MKC, -1.50%, the parent of flavor brands including French's, Frank's RedHot and McCormick spices, reported Thursday fiscal second-quarter profit that beat expectations and raised its full-year outlook, while sales came up just shy of forecasts as volume fell. Net income for the quarter to May 31 rose to $152.1 million, or 56 cents a share, from $118.5 million, or 44 cents a share, in the year-ago period.

title: McCormick promotes Brendan Foley to CEO
text: McCormick & Co. Inc. MKC, -1.50% said late Wednesday that its board has promoted Brendan Foley to the role of chief executive officer, effective Sept. 1. Foley was also elected to the board, effective immediately.

title: McCormick (MKC) Gears Up for Q2 Earnings: Things to Note
text: McCormick's (MKC) second-quarter results are likely to reflect gains from the Comprehensive Continuous Improvement program amid cost inflation.

title: Q2 profits are set for their biggest drop since the pandemic, but then the worst may be over for the year
text: Wall Street is expecting a third straight quarter of falling profits and the worst decrease since the pandemic in 2020, when second quarter earnings are reported in July, but that may be the bottom for this year.
